subject: Project Management
title: The Software Crisis
description: A fast breakdown of The Software Crisis for builders.

hook.text: In the 1960s, software projects failed so often that experts called it a 'crisis.'

buildup.text: Projects ran over budget, missed deadlines, and delivered software nobody wanted.

discovery.text: A 1968 NATO conference gave it a name: the 'software crisis.' The industry needed help.

twist.text: The proposed fix — more documentation and rigid planning — often made things worse.

climax.text: For decades, the industry tried to solve software with more process. It didn't work.

punchline.text: They tried to plan their way out. The crisis deepened.

quiz.question: When was the 'software crisis' formally named?
options: At the Agile Manifesto meeting in 2001
